title: One of the richest self-made women in America grew up working in her family’s restaurant—now she’s worth $6.9 billion – NBC 5 Dallas-Fort Worth
text:
[# Money Report In partnership with](https://www.nbcdfw.com/news/business/money-report/)

[news](https://www.nbcdfw.com/tag/news/)

# One of the richest self-made women in America grew up working in her family's restaurant—now she's worth $6.9 billion

#### By Megan Sauer, CNBC • Published June 7, 2025 • Updated on June 7, 2025 at 8:12 am

BOOKMARKER

[](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.nbcdfw.com%2Fnews%2Fbusiness%2Fmoney-report%2Fone-of-the-richest-self-made-women-in-america-grew-up-working-in-her-familys-restaurant-now-shes-worth-6-9-billion%2F3858342%2F&One%20of%20the%20richest%20self-made%20women%20in%20America%20grew%20up%20working%20in%20her%20family%27s%20restaurant%E2%80%94now%20she%27s%20worth%20%246.9%20billion?_osource=db_npd_nbc_kxas_fb_shr) 

Pool | Getty Images Sport | Getty Images

DETROIT, MI – MARCH 08: Detroit Red Wings owner Marian Ilitch talks to some of her players during the public visitation of NHL Hall of Famer and former Detroit Red Wing Ted Lindsay at Little Caesars Arena home of the Detroit Red Wings on March 8, 2019 in Detroit, Michigan. (Photo by Dave Reginek – Pool/Getty Images)

Little Caesars co-founder Marian Ilitch is the third-richest self-made woman in the U.S., according to a new Forbes list.

Ilitch has an estimated net worth of $6.9 billion, [Forbes reported on Tuesday](https://www.forbes.com/profile/marian-ilitch/) . Most of that wealth likely comes from the Little Caesars pizza restaurant chain, which she co-founded with her late husband Michael Ilitch in Garden City, Michigan, in 1959. Little Caesars brings in an estimated $5 billion in annual systemwide sales, Forbes estimated.

Watch NBC 5 free wherever you are

[WATCH HERE](https://www.nbcdfw.com/watch/?_osource=ed_npd_nbc_dfw_site_fast_auto2)

Ilitch, 90, took the reins as chairwoman of her family's holding company Ilitch Holdings, which owns Little Caesars, after her husband died in 2017.

Growing up, Ilitch refilled napkin dispensers and salt and pepper shakers in her father's restaurant, [according to Ilitch Holdings' website](https://www.ilitchholdings.com/marian-ilitch-bio.html) . As an adult, she worked in retail and as a reservation clerk for Delta Air Lines before getting married and becoming a full-time mother. The couple invested their family savings to open the first Little Caesars location, the website says.

Create a free account with NBC 5 DFW to save articles and videos.

[LEARN MORE](https://www.nbcdfw.com/news/local/how-to-create-account-save-articles-nbc-5/3865937/)

_**DON'T MISS:**_ [_**A step-by-step guide to buying your first home—and avoiding costly mistakes**_](https://smarter.cnbcmakeit.com/p/how-to-buy-your-first-home?utm_source=cnbc&utm_medium=makeitarticle&utm_campaign=body)

Little Caesars opened its first franchised location in Warren, Michigan, in 1962, and grew rapidly from there: It opened its 50th location seven years later, the company's website says. It has thousands of locations worldwide, with roughly 80% of them [franchise-owned](https://franchise.littlecaesars.com/) , according to the company.

Other parts of the Ilitch family's wealth come from their investments in the city of Detroit. Ilitch and her husband bought the Detroit Red Wings for a [reported $8 million in 1982](https://www.freep.com/story/sports/nhl/red-wings/2022/06/03/detroit-red-wings-turned-around-40-years-ago-sale-mike-ilitch/7489078001/) . Her husband also bought the Detroit Tigers from Dominos Pizza founder Tom Monaghan for [a reported $85 million](https://www.freep.com/story/sports/mlb/tigers/2017/02/18/detroit-tigers-ilitch-sale-price/97908642/) in 1992.

The couple purchased the Fox Theater, located in the heart of the city, in 1987 — and spent $12.5 million restoring it, [according to the Detroit News](https://www.detroitnews.com/story/business/2017/02/11/ilitch-olympia-entertainment/97813522/) . And Ilitch owns Detroit's MotorCity Casino Hotel, first investing in it and then buying out her partners for $525 million in 2005, [according to ESPN](https://www.espn.com/nhl/news/story?id=2036579) .

Some of the Ilitches' efforts to invest in the city sparked controversy. The family promised a $200 million development around Little Caesars Arena, which opened in September 2017 — but some locals were skeptical the investment actually helped residents, The Guardian [reported](https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2018/oct/08/detroit-the-district-redevelopment-ilitch-companies) in October 2018.

Ilitch has generally avoided the spotlight throughout her adult life, as did her husband. Notably, Michael quietly helped pay rent for civil rights icon Rosa Parks during the 1990s, after Parks moved into an apartment in downtown Detroit, the [Sports Business Journal reported in 2014](https://www.sportsbusinessjournal.com/Journal/Issues/2014/02/24/Champions/Ilitch-Rosa-Parks/) .

"It's important that people know what Mr. Mike Ilitch did for Ms. Rosa Parks because it's symbolic of what he has always done for the people of our city," Damon Keith, a former circuit judge on the 6th U.S. Court of Appeals who connected Parks with Michael, told the publication.

"You don't find people like Mike and Marian often, who want to help when they're needed," Keith [said to the Washington Post](https://www.washingtonpost.com/news/inspired-life/wp/2017/02/16/mike-ilitch-was-famous-for-his-fortune-but-his-surprising-connection-to-rosa-parks-reveals-something-more/) in February 2017.
